template_name: Standard Prescription Format - Oral Thrush in Neonate

condition.name: Oral Thrush (Neonatal Candidiasis)
overview: Oral thrush is a common fungal infection in neonates caused by Candida albicans.
Presents as white adherent plaques over tongue, gums, inner cheeks and palate.
Usually benign but may cause feeding difficulty and irritability.

oral_thrush: May leave erythematous or bleeding base

history_template: Onset and duration of white patches
examination_template: Inspect oral cavity: tongue, gums, inner cheeks, palate
Look for erythema or bleeding after removal
Look for systemic signs if severe
red_flags: Extensive thrush spreading beyond oral cavity

topical_first_line.dose: Apply thin layer over affected areas
topical_first_line.duration: 7–14 days or until 48 hours after resolution

alternative_options.dose: 1 mL applied inside cheeks, tongue, gums

alternative_options.note: Use cautiously if choking risk absent

supportive_measures: Clean oral cavity gently after feeds
hygiene_measures: Sterilize bottles, nipples and pacifiers daily
Treat maternal nipple candidiasis if breastfeeding

clinical_clues_summary.feature: Extensive lesions or poor weight gain

flow_logic: If easily removable → likely milk residue
If severe or no improvement → consider systemic therapy or further evaluation
quick_checklist_for_opd: Are white patches adherent to mucosa?
Do they not wipe off easily?
parent_counseling: Oral thrush is common and easily treatable. — मुंह का छाला आम है और आसानी से ठीक हो जाता है।
It is not due to poor care. — यह खराब देखभाल की वजह से नहीं होता।
Maintain proper hygiene of feeding equipment. — दूध पिलाने के बर्तन साफ़ रखें।
Complete full course of medication even if symptoms improve early. — लक्षण कम होने पर भी दवाई पूरी करें।
Consult doctor if baby develops fever, feeding refusal or spreading lesions. — बुखार, दूध न पीना, या छाले बढ़ना दिखे तो डॉक्टर को दिखाएं।

standard_prescription_format.title: Standard Prescription - Oral Thrush in Neonate

medication_template.duration: 7–14 days or 48 hrs after symptom resolution
medication_template.instructions_en: Apply gently over tongue, cheeks, gums after feeding
medication_template.instructions_hi: दूध पिलाने के बाद जीभ, गाल और मसूड़ों पर हल्के से लगाएं
गालों और जीभ के अंदर लगाएं

hygiene_advice: Sterilize feeding bottles and pacifiers daily
Treat maternal nipple infection if present

warning_signs: Feeding refusal or excessive crying during feeds — दूध पीने से मना करना या दूध पीते समय बहुत रोना
Spreading lesions beyond mouth — छाले मुंह से बाहर फैलना
Poor weight gain — वज़न न बढ़ना
Persistent or recurring infection despite treatment — इलाज के बावजूद बार-बार होना
Rash in diaper area — डायपर वाली जगह पर दाने
expected_course: Oral thrush in neonates usually responds well to topical antifungal therapy (clotrimazole or nystatin) within 7-14 days. Treatment should continue for 48 hours after resolution of lesions. Recurrence is common if hygiene measures are not followed or if maternal nipple candidiasis is untreated.
key_clinical_points: White adherent plaques that do not wipe off easily are the hallmark of oral thrush.
Milk residue is easily wiped away and does not leave erythema underneath.
Clotrimazole 1% mouth paint or Nystatin oral suspension are first-line treatments.
Always treat concurrent maternal nipple candidiasis to prevent reinfection.
Sterilize all feeding equipment (bottles, nipples, pacifiers) daily.
Systemic fluconazole is reserved for refractory or systemic candidiasis under specialist guidance.

severity_assessment.mild: Few white patches, feeding unaffected, no systemic symptoms — topical antifungal and hygiene advice.
severity_assessment.moderate: Multiple patches, some feeding difficulty or irritability — topical antifungal, close follow-up.
severity_assessment.severe: Extensive lesions, feeding refusal, failure to thrive, systemic symptoms — consider systemic therapy, rule out immunodeficiency.
